data.body:
PASS 1 review — [T3] Integration feasibility (overlay): NOT APPLICABLE / PASS.

T3 applies only when a plan integrates an EXTERNAL API/CLI/service/library or asserts a capability it has not used before. This follow-up plan ("Move .bridge_state/ + reconciler lock files off the shared tickets branch") is wholly internal git-branch hygiene: it relies on git mechanisms already exercised in-repo — gitignore scaffolding (init.py `_GITIGNORE`/`_commit_gitignore`), union merge with `git merge --allow-unrelated-histories` (sync.py `_union_merge`/`_do_reconverge`), the `merge=ours` `.gitattributes` driver (init.py `_ensure_merge_ours_driver`, parent epic WU-3), and `git update-ref` CAS ref-advance + `git show tickets:<file>` reads (_advisory_lock.py:172-214, 491-586). No external subcommand/endpoint, no new library, no auth/HTTPS surface, and no asserted-but-unverified capability is introduced.

Per the overlay's ANTI-FP guidance ("an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable"), no T3 findings are raised. The replacement cross-clone coordination design is genuinely under-specified, but that gap is a distributed-coordination/prior-art concern (already flagged under T1/A1/G1G2 above), not an external-integration-feasibility one.
